nodes.title: Main Street Renovation Project Breaks Ground

body.processed:
The City of Maplewood has officially broken ground on the $12 million Main Street renovation project. The project will transform the downtown corridor with new sidewalks, bike lanes, improved lighting, and upgraded water infrastructure.

"This project represents our commitment to creating a vibrant, walkable downtown that serves residents and attracts businesses," said Mayor Patricia Chen at the groundbreaking ceremony.

Construction is expected to take 18 months, with phased closures to minimize disruption to local businesses. The project is funded through a combination of federal infrastructure grants and city bonds.
body.summary: The City of Maplewood has officially broken ground on the $12 million Main Street renovation project. The project will transform the downtown corridor with new sidewalks, bike lanes, improved lighting

body.processed:
City Manager David Rodriguez has presented the proposed FY2027 budget of $142 million to the City Council. The budget prioritizes public safety, infrastructure maintenance, and parks improvements while maintaining the current tax rate.

Key highlights include funding for 12 new police officers, a $5 million road resurfacing program, and $3.2 million for the new Riverside Park renovation. Public comment on the budget will be accepted through April 30.

Budget documents are available for review at City Hall, the public library, and online at the city website.

body.processed:
The City of Maplewood invites all residents to the grand opening celebration of the newly renovated Riverside Park on Saturday, May 15. The $3.2 million renovation includes new playgrounds, a splash pad, walking trails, and an outdoor amphitheater.

The celebration will feature live music, food trucks, kids activities, and guided tours of the new facilities. Free parking is available at the community center lot.
